config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I believe individual rights should be the starting point, because society is healthiest when people are protected from being sacrificed for vague collective goals. Of course, rights are not absolute and can be limited to prevent real harm to others, but “society first” can easily become an excuse for abuse, censorship, or discrimination. In my view, a good society protects individuals precisely so the whole community can thrive.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan daripada hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika “kebaikan bersama” dipakai tanpa batas yang jelas, itu sering berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk menekan kebebasan berbicara, kebebasan beragama, dan perlindungan minoritas. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi pembatasannya harus sempit, jelas, dan benar-benar untuk mencegah kerugian nyata, bukan sekadar demi tujuan kolektif yang kabur. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi orang per orang sambil tetap menjaga ketertiban umum.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “the common good” can be used to silence criticism or pressure minorities. Community matters a lot in our culture, but that does not mean the state or majority gets a blank check to override basic freedoms whenever it is convenient. I do think individual rights can be limited when there is real harm to others, but those limits must be narrow, fair, and accountable. A healthy society is not one that sacrifices people for an abstract collective; it is one that protects people well enough that the collective can genuinely be strong.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Amerika saya melihat “kepentingan masyarakat” sering dipakai terlalu longgar untuk membenarkan pengekangan kebebasan individu. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi, tetapi hanya ketika ada kerugian nyata dan aturan yang jelas, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa itu lebih praktis atau lebih tertib. Argumen bahwa masyarakat menjadi kuat kalau orang per orang dilindungi menurut saya jauh lebih meyakinkan daripada gagasan bahwa individu harus dikorbankan demi tujuan kolektif yang kabur. Kalau prioritasnya dibalik, yang sering terjadi bukan solidaritas, melainkan penyalahgunaan kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the statement as a general rule, because in Indonesia I have seen how “society first” can be used to pressure dissenters and minorities while pretending it is for harmony. Community is important in our culture, but that does not give the majority or the state a moral blank check to override speech, belief, or personal dignity whenever it is convenient. I do think individual rights can be limited for real harm and public safety, but those limits must be narrow, transparent, and accountable. A society is stronger when it protects people first, not when it treats individuals as disposable for vague collective goals.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai sebagai alasan kabur untuk membatasi kebebasan berbicara, beragama, dan melindungi minoritas. Saya tidak menyangkal bahwa hak individu bisa dibatasi, tetapi pembatasannya harus benar-benar untuk mencegah kerugian nyata dan harus jelas batasnya, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa itu lebih rapi atau lebih nyaman. Justru ketika individu dilindungi dengan serius, masyarakat biasanya menjadi lebih kuat dan lebih adil. Kalau prioritas dibalik, yang sering muncul bukan kebaikan bersama, melainkan penyalahgunaan kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
